sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Abnormal lab findings and dizziness.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports being in the ER due to a low hemoglobin and experiencing episodes of dizziness and lightheadedness. He denies recent trauma and reports a slight nick from a knife while chopping onions, but no blood in his stools or bleeding. He reports a slightly decreased appetite, though he wishes he had some weight loss. He states that sitting at the back of a car sometimes causes nausea. He has a knee replacement that is doing okay, walking without problems. He recalls a previous possible lung cancer scare a few years ago, which was benign. He also mentions a bit of nausea, particularly when sitting.
Findings
Physical exam reveals no distress, a regular heart rhythm, clear lung sounds, and tenderness to the right lower quadrant upon palpation. Lower extremities are without edema. Hemoglobin is 8.2. The endoscopy revealed gastritis and a slight polyp, which was biopsied and the results are pending. The nurse reports the complete blood count will be ordered.
Assessment
The patient presents with anemia, likely secondary to gastritis and a possible polyp, as evidenced by the low hemoglobin and endoscopic findings.
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: I am ordering protonix, 40 milligrams, once a day to help with the gastritis. I recommend reducing caffeine intake and avoiding ibuprofen and alcohol. I will also order a complete blood count. We will schedule a follow-up appointment in two weeks to assess the response to treatment and review the biopsy results.
